metadata.name: Japan Briefing
metadata.description: Daily news briefing for the NYT Tokyo Bureau Chief

all.headline: 欠航続いたドバイからの便が羽田に到着 イラン 攻撃応酬で影響
all.description: アメリカなどとイランとの間で攻撃の応酬が続き、日本と中東を結ぶ空の便への影響が広がる中、欠航が続いていたUAE＝アラブ首長国連邦のドバイからの便が運航を再開し、5日夜、羽田空港に到着しました。

all.headline: イラスエル軍 “イランに13回目の波状攻撃” イランも攻撃継続
all.description: アメリカとイスラエルによるイランへの攻撃は連日続いていて、イスラエル軍は、5日、イラン各地に13回目の波状攻撃を行ったと発表しました。一方、イランも攻撃を続けていて、5日には隣国のアゼルバイジャンがイランから無人機攻撃を受けたと発表し、イラン側が否定するなど地域に影響が広がっています。

all.headline: トランプ大統領 国土安全保障省長官を解任 移民摘発を管轄
all.description: アメリカのトランプ大統領は滞在資格のない移民の大規模な摘発を行ってきたICE＝移民税関捜査局を管轄する国土安全保障省のノーム長官を解任することを明らかにしました。

all.headline: NYダウ 一時1100ドル超値下がり 原油市場 先物は値上がり高値
all.description: 5日のニューヨーク株式市場では売り注文が広がり、ダウ平均株価が前日の終値から一時、1100ドルを超える値下がりとなりました。イラン情勢を受けて原油の先物価格が上昇したことで投資家の間では世界経済の減速に対する警戒感が高まっています。

all.headline: トヨタ 中東向け輸出の自動車 国内生産を約2万台減産へ
all.description: イラン情勢をめぐって緊張が続く中、トヨタ自動車が、今月末まで、中東向けに輸出する自動車の国内での生産をおよそ2万台減産することがわかりました。海上輸送に影響が出ていることを踏まえた対応とみられ、ほかのメーカーにも同様の影響が広がることが懸念されます。

all.headline: 米ヘグセス国防長官 中国念頭に“南北アメリカでの結束を”
all.description: アメリカのヘグセス国防長官は、中国を念頭に、アメリカを除外して中南米の国々と連携を強めようとする動きがあると指摘した上で、「国益の観点から地理的な結びつきを重視すべきだ」として、南北アメリカでの結束を訴えました。

all.headline: WBC 日本が「世界一」目指し 台湾と初戦へ 山本由伸が先発
all.description: WBC＝ワールド・ベースボール・クラシックで日本は、6日夜、初戦となる1次ラウンドの台湾戦に臨みます。ドジャースの大谷翔平選手など、過去最多となる8人の大リーガーを擁する日本の「世界一」に挑む戦いがいよいよ始まります。

all.headline: Japanese startup's rocket launch fails in 3rd attempt as flight aborted
all.description: A Japanese startup aborted the flight of its rocket Thursday just over a minute after launch from a space complex in western JapanRead full story here

all.headline: Japan to evacuate nationals from war-hit Middle East on chartered aircraft
all.description: Japan will evacuate its nationals from four Middle East countries on chartered aircraft, the Japanese Foreign Ministry said ThursdRead full story here

all.headline: S. Korea issues joint AI supply chain guidance with global cybersecurity agencies
all.description: SEOUL - The National Intelligence Service (NIS) said Thursday it has issued a joint guidance on artificial intelligence (AI)Read full story here

all.headline: The long, humble history of the umbrella
all.description: From ancient courts to Meiji streets, the umbrella shelters commerce, faith, folly and fragile human longing.

all.headline: Japan Times 1976: Bomb explosion in Sapporo claims 2 lives, injures 85
all.description: A note left after the terrorist attack at a government building cited "imperialists" as the target.

all.headline: Adnoc boss and UAE minister Al Jaber meets Sanae Takaichi
all.description: "While your energy security continues to be our priority, we will not be able to do much if there is any distortion in the Strait of Hormuz,” Al Jaber said.

all.headline: 3,827 teacher positions unfilled across Japan: 2025 survey
all.description: The figure jumped from 2,065 in the previous survey four years earlier, reflecting the country's worsening teacher shortage.

all.headline: Japan raises travel alert for six countries in Middle East
all.description: The alert is now at Level 3, the second highest in the ministry's four-tier system.

all.headline: Australian baseball icon Tim Kennelly prepares to take final bow at WBC
all.description: Kennelly is arguably the greatest player in the history of the Australian Baseball League.

all.headline: Reeling from loss, Niigata’s animation festival refuses to fold
all.description: After losing key organizers and the death of its founder, the festival wasn’t sure it could go on. Instead of going dark, however, NIAFF rebuilt from scratch.

all.headline: LDP drops proposal to conduct fiscal 2026 budget deliberations on Saturday
all.description: The ruling party had proposed the weekend session in response to opposition concerns that time spent deliberating the fiscal 2026 budget bill had been insufficient.

all.headline: Oil refiners in Japan ask government to tap strategic reserves
all.description: Japan gets more than 90% of its oil from the Middle East, leaving it vulnerable to the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz that’s preventing energy exports.

all.headline: Japan’s national museums to implement dual-pricing system
all.description: The planned change, a first for state-run museums, will hike admission fees for nonresidents by March 2031.

all.description: It is also the first time the target has been lowered since it was cut to "around 5%" in 2023.

all.headline: Veteran Bihar chief minister to step down for move to parliament
all.description: Nitish Kumar says the state's new government will have his full cooperation and guidance.

all.headline: Global order is 'breaking down', Carney tells Australian parliament
all.description: The Canadian prime minister said Australia and Canada should work together as 'strategic cousins'.

all.headline: South Korea woman and doctors guilty of murder of newborn baby
all.description: The case has gripped South Korea, where there is no regulation on how far into pregnancy an abortion can take place.

all.headline: Japanese court upholds order to dissolve Unification Church
all.description: The church popularly known as Moonies came under scrutiny after the 2022 killing of ex-PM Shinzo Abe.

all.headline: Kathmandu empties as about 800,000 Nepalis head home to vote
all.description: Authorities have reported hours-long jams over the weekend while photos show  buses lining highways.

all.description: The Met Police suspect three men of assisting Chinese intelligence services in the UK.

all.headline: Australian girl, 8, killed in snowmobile accident in Japan
all.description: It is the fourth Australian death at a Japanese ski resort this year.

all.headline: The battle to save Sri Lanka's leopards

all.headline: Wildlife is flourishing around Fukushima
all.description: Nearly a decade after Japan's Fukushima nuclear disaster, researchers have discovered that wildlife is thriving in the areas evacuated by humans, despite radiological contamination.

all.headline: China debuts 620 kph train prototype
all.description: China has revealed a prototype for a new high-speed Maglev train that is capable of reaching speeds of 620 kilometers (385 miles) per hour.

all.headline: A Shanghai building 'walks' to a new location
all.description: Shanghai residents passing through the city's eastern Huangpu district in October might have stumbled upon an unusual sight: a "walking" building.

all.headline: Self-driving robotaxis are taking off in China
all.description: The world has been inching toward fully autonomous cars for years. In China, one company just got even closer to making it a reality.

national.headline: 欠航続いたドバイからの便が羽田に到着 イラン 攻撃応酬で影響
national.description: アメリカなどとイランとの間で攻撃の応酬が続き、日本と中東を結ぶ空の便への影響が広がる中、欠航が続いていたUAE＝アラブ首長国連邦のドバイからの便が運航を再開し、5日夜、羽田空港に到着しました。

national.headline: イラスエル軍 “イランに13回目の波状攻撃” イランも攻撃継続
national.description: アメリカとイスラエルによるイランへの攻撃は連日続いていて、イスラエル軍は、5日、イラン各地に13回目の波状攻撃を行ったと発表しました。一方、イランも攻撃を続けていて、5日には隣国のアゼルバイジャンがイランから無人機攻撃を受けたと発表し、イラン側が否定するなど地域に影響が広がっています。

national.headline: トランプ大統領 国土安全保障省長官を解任 移民摘発を管轄
national.description: アメリカのトランプ大統領は滞在資格のない移民の大規模な摘発を行ってきたICE＝移民税関捜査局を管轄する国土安全保障省のノーム長官を解任することを明らかにしました。

national.headline: NYダウ 一時1100ドル超値下がり 原油市場 先物は値上がり高値
national.description: 5日のニューヨーク株式市場では売り注文が広がり、ダウ平均株価が前日の終値から一時、1100ドルを超える値下がりとなりました。イラン情勢を受けて原油の先物価格が上昇したことで投資家の間では世界経済の減速に対する警戒感が高まっています。

national.headline: トヨタ 中東向け輸出の自動車 国内生産を約2万台減産へ
national.description: イラン情勢をめぐって緊張が続く中、トヨタ自動車が、今月末まで、中東向けに輸出する自動車の国内での生産をおよそ2万台減産することがわかりました。海上輸送に影響が出ていることを踏まえた対応とみられ、ほかのメーカーにも同様の影響が広がることが懸念されます。

national.headline: 米ヘグセス国防長官 中国念頭に“南北アメリカでの結束を”
national.description: アメリカのヘグセス国防長官は、中国を念頭に、アメリカを除外して中南米の国々と連携を強めようとする動きがあると指摘した上で、「国益の観点から地理的な結びつきを重視すべきだ」として、南北アメリカでの結束を訴えました。

national.headline: WBC 日本が「世界一」目指し 台湾と初戦へ 山本由伸が先発
national.description: WBC＝ワールド・ベースボール・クラシックで日本は、6日夜、初戦となる1次ラウンドの台湾戦に臨みます。ドジャースの大谷翔平選手など、過去最多となる8人の大リーガーを擁する日本の「世界一」に挑む戦いがいよいよ始まります。

national.headline: The long, humble history of the umbrella
national.description: From ancient courts to Meiji streets, the umbrella shelters commerce, faith, folly and fragile human longing.

national.headline: Japan Times 1976: Bomb explosion in Sapporo claims 2 lives, injures 85
national.description: A note left after the terrorist attack at a government building cited "imperialists" as the target.

national.headline: Adnoc boss and UAE minister Al Jaber meets Sanae Takaichi
national.description: "While your energy security continues to be our priority, we will not be able to do much if there is any distortion in the Strait of Hormuz,” Al Jaber said.

national.headline: 3,827 teacher positions unfilled across Japan: 2025 survey
national.description: The figure jumped from 2,065 in the previous survey four years earlier, reflecting the country's worsening teacher shortage.

national.headline: Japan raises travel alert for six countries in Middle East
national.description: The alert is now at Level 3, the second highest in the ministry's four-tier system.

national.headline: Australian baseball icon Tim Kennelly prepares to take final bow at WBC
national.description: Kennelly is arguably the greatest player in the history of the Australian Baseball League.

national.headline: Reeling from loss, Niigata’s animation festival refuses to fold
national.description: After losing key organizers and the death of its founder, the festival wasn’t sure it could go on. Instead of going dark, however, NIAFF rebuilt from scratch.

national.headline: LDP drops proposal to conduct fiscal 2026 budget deliberations on Saturday
national.description: The ruling party had proposed the weekend session in response to opposition concerns that time spent deliberating the fiscal 2026 budget bill had been insufficient.

national.headline: Oil refiners in Japan ask government to tap strategic reserves
national.description: Japan gets more than 90% of its oil from the Middle East, leaving it vulnerable to the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz that’s preventing energy exports.

national.headline: Japan’s national museums to implement dual-pricing system
national.description: The planned change, a first for state-run museums, will hike admission fees for nonresidents by March 2031.

wire.description: A Japanese startup aborted the flight of its rocket Thursday just over a minute after launch from a space complex in western JapanRead full story here

competitors.headline: Nepalis vote in first election since Gen Z uprising for change
competitors.description: This election will test whether the country is ready for a new, untested generation to shape its future.

competitors.headline: China sets lowest economic growth target since 1991
competitors.description: It is also the first time the target has been lowered since it was cut to "around 5%" in 2023.

competitors.headline: Veteran Bihar chief minister to step down for move to parliament
competitors.description: Nitish Kumar says the state's new government will have his full cooperation and guidance.

competitors.headline: Global order is 'breaking down', Carney tells Australian parliament
competitors.description: The Canadian prime minister said Australia and Canada should work together as 'strategic cousins'.

competitors.headline: South Korea woman and doctors guilty of murder of newborn baby
competitors.description: The case has gripped South Korea, where there is no regulation on how far into pregnancy an abortion can take place.

competitors.headline: Japanese court upholds order to dissolve Unification Church
competitors.description: The church popularly known as Moonies came under scrutiny after the 2022 killing of ex-PM Shinzo Abe.

competitors.headline: Kathmandu empties as about 800,000 Nepalis head home to vote
competitors.description: Authorities have reported hours-long jams over the weekend while photos show  buses lining highways.

competitors.headline: Partner of UK MP arrested on suspicion of spying for China, BBC told
competitors.description: The Met Police suspect three men of assisting Chinese intelligence services in the UK.

competitors.headline: Australian girl, 8, killed in snowmobile accident in Japan
competitors.description: It is the fourth Australian death at a Japanese ski resort this year.

competitors.headline: India's top court angry after junior judge cites fake AI-generated orders
competitors.description: In several recent instances, AI has disrupted court proceedings in India and elsewhere.

competitors.headline: How Indonesia's new sex laws will affect tourists
competitors.description: International travelers have been flocking back to the popular resort island of Bali as the Covid pandemic subsides, prompting hopes that Indonesia's battered tourism industry is on the road to recove

competitors.headline: Afghanistan
North Korea
This 'edible' plastic bag alternative could save Nara's sacred deer
competitors.description: A local entrepreneur in the Japanese tourist destination of Nara has developed an alternative to plastic shopping bags, to protect the town's sacred deer.

competitors.headline: The battle to save Sri Lanka's leopards
competitors.description: Having endured a bloody civil war from 1983 until 2009, Sri Lanka is now at peace and developing rapidly. That's good news for the country as a whole, but the island nation's leopards are under threat

competitors.headline: Wildlife is flourishing around Fukushima
competitors.description: Nearly a decade after Japan's Fukushima nuclear disaster, researchers have discovered that wildlife is thriving in the areas evacuated by humans, despite radiological contamination.

competitors.headline: China debuts 620 kph train prototype
competitors.description: China has revealed a prototype for a new high-speed Maglev train that is capable of reaching speeds of 620 kilometers (385 miles) per hour.

competitors.headline: A Shanghai building 'walks' to a new location
competitors.description: Shanghai residents passing through the city's eastern Huangpu district in October might have stumbled upon an unusual sight: a "walking" building.

competitors.headline: Self-driving robotaxis are taking off in China
competitors.description: The world has been inching toward fully autonomous cars for years. In China, one company just got even closer to making it a reality.

competitors.headline: Travel to Japan during Covid-19: What you need to know
competitors.description: If you're planning a trip to Japan, here's what you'll need to know and expect if you want to visit during the global coronavirus pandemic.

screenshots.name: Takaichi Sanae
tweets: In light of the deteriorating situation in the Middle East, today we have ordered that the danger information level be raised to Level 3 (recommendation to refrain from travel) for countries surrounding Iran that are actually under attack, specifically Kuwait, Saudi Arabia (eastern), Bahrain, Qatar, the United Arab Emirates, and Oman.

I attended the award ceremony for the Japan Medical Association Red Beard Award.

The award recipients are modern-day "Dr. Redbeards" who have supported the lives of residents in the medical field for many years as family doctors in their areas.

Today, we received a ``proposal regarding the fundamental strengthening of Japan's intelligence function'' from the Intelligence Strategy Headquarters of the Liberal Democratic Party Political Affairs Research Group.

I would like to thank you from the bottom of my heart for compiling such meaningful recommendations that indicate concrete policy directions.
I heard that a virtual currency called SANAE TOKEN has been issued and a certain amount of transactions are taking place.
